applied_fixes.title: SECRET_KEY generator produced 16 chars/~44 bits of entropy, not 64/256
applied_fixes.fix: Replaced Get-Random -Count 64 (sampling-without-replacement over a fixed 16-item set) with RNGCryptoServiceProvider.GetBytes over 32 bytes, hex-encoded to 64 chars. Verified independently by direct reproduction of both the bug and the fix.

applied_fixes.title: Generated CLI shims discarded the return value of comment/phase0-seal/weights, always exiting 0
applied_fixes.fix: Wrapped every shim's -c invocation in sys.exit(<function>()) instead of a bare <function>() call. Verified independently: python -c "def f(): return 1\nf()" exits 0; python -c "...sys.exit(f())" exits 1.

applied_fixes.title: _rmtree_long_path_safe had no retry for locked files, despite exactly this failure being hit during this feature's own development
applied_fixes.fix: Added bounded retry-with-backoff (5 attempts, 1s delay) on PermissionError before raising BuildError.

applied_fixes.title: Subprocess calls decoding child text output without errors=replace / PYTHONIOENCODING, reproducing an already-fixed encoding-crash bug class
applied_fixes.reviewer: reliability, kieran-python, ce-learnings-researcher (cross-reviewer agreement, 3 independent reviewers)
applied_fixes.fix: Added a local _utf8_child_env() helper (mirrors backlink_publisher._util.subprocess_env.utf8_child_env without importing the package) and applied errors="replace" + PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 to all 6 subprocess.run call sites.

applied_fixes.title: Network-touching downloads and subprocess calls had no timeout, could hang the build indefinitely
applied_fixes.fix: Added _NETWORK_TIMEOUT_S (300s) to all download/pip/npm/pip-audit calls and _LOCAL_SUBPROCESS_TIMEOUT_S (60s) to the local CLI-shim existence check. Switched _http_get_to_file from urlretrieve (no timeout parameter) to urlopen(timeout=...).

applied_fixes.title: pip-audit fallback install directory (tempfile.mkdtemp()) was never cleaned up
applied_fixes.fix: Switched to tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() context manager; extracted _run_pip_audit_cmd() helper to keep both call sites consistent.

applied_fixes.title: _create_zip_and_checksum deleted any pre-existing zip before confirming the new archive built successfully
applied_fixes.fix: Builds to a <pkg_name>.staging.zip name, computes the checksum, then atomically os.replace()s onto the real path -- the last-known-good zip is never deleted until its replacement is proven good.

applied_fixes.title: Plan doc missing required claims: {} frontmatter (CI-blocking per plan-claims-gate)
applied_fixes.fix: Added claims: {} with the same opt-out rationale style as the sibling same-day plan doc.

residual_actionable_work.title: build_windows_package.py (~1350 SLOC) is a monolith-split candidate along its own Unit 1/2/3/5 section boundaries
residual_actionable_work.note: Not CI-enforced (monolith_budget.toml only scans src/backlink_publisher, webui_app, webui_store) -- a design choice for a future pass, not a blocker.

residual_actionable_work.title: provision_interpreter's output_dir means python-embed/ itself; generate_cli_shims' output_dir means the package root -- same param name, opposite semantics
residual_actionable_work.note: A real footgun for future maintainers despite thorough docstrings; a rename touches multiple call sites so was deferred rather than rushed.

residual_actionable_work.title: Unit 1's provision_interpreter success path (extract -> patch _pth -> pip bootstrap -> install -> catalog check -> pip-audit) has no fast/mocked test, only the network+Windows-gated real e2e test
CLI shim existence-check defaults to sys.executable (build host) rather than the shipped python-embed interpreter for 48 of 49 commands; real e2e only exercises bp.bat end-to-end
Generated CLI shims and setup-scheduler/install-playwright/setup-wizard templates have no python.exe-missing guard or BP_NONINTERACTIVE bypass, unlike the hand-written launch-webui/launch-cli scripts
launch-webui.bat.tmpl's port-detection has a TOCTOU race between the probe and the real bind on double-launch
MAX_PATH is only guarded at build time (_rmtree_long_path_safe); the shipped runtime (deep extraction paths, CJK usernames) has no equivalent guard
Maintainer build CLI collapses every BuildError to exit code 1 rather than typed exit codes matching _util.errors' convention
e2e test's taskkill cleanup doesn't check its own exit code before waiting, risking an orphaned server process on a taskkill failure
Duplicated test boilerplate (_REAL_ENV, _real_packaging_build_enabled(), REPO_ROOT) copy-pasted across all 4 test files; no tests/packaging/conftest.py
Zip-archiving step (shutil.make_archive) not wrapped with the same long-path-safe technique as the rmtree operations in the same file

advisory_only: CLI shim %* argument passthrough has no escaping for cmd.exe-special characters (percent signs, embedded quotes) -- documented parity gap, not fixed.

get-pip.py bootstrap and pip-installed transitive dependencies are not individually pinned/audited beyond the pip-audit gate on the final install -- accepted residual risk, already documented in the plan's Risks table.

subprocess.run calls don't pin stdin=DEVNULL; a wrapped tool (pip/npm) attempting an interactive read with no attached TTY could hang silently instead of failing fast. Low-priority defense-in-depth (agent-native review).

setup-wizard.bat.tmpl's Y/N prompt is a legitimate human-only convenience wrapper -- launch-webui.bat itself has zero interactive prompts and is the true non-interactive/agent-drivable entrypoint (agent-native review, verdict PASS).
testing_gaps: No test extracts the built package to a path with spaces/apostrophe/CJK characters, despite the target docs being entirely Traditional Chinese.
Real e2e test exercises only 1 of 49 CLI shims (bp.bat) end-to-end against the real shipped interpreter.
No test simulates two concurrent launch-webui.bat invocations to catch the port-detection race.
No test covers a pre-existing valid .zip surviving a failed rebuild attempt (the exact scenario the zip-staging fix addresses).
